mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase borrowing costs and discount rates, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of rate-hike instances.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, which forces corporate earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations yield negative forward returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization and operational efficiency, which boosts short-term market sentiment and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day returns in > 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above the 200.0 threshold trigger expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases equity discount rates and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 200.0 yields positive forward returns in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit expectations and equity valuations, resulting in positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (p > 0.10) across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals immediate supply chain disruptions and weakening manufacturing output, which triggers risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Sharp industrial production declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in > 55% of cases.
